Title: Amylopectin branch trimming and biosynthesis elucidated by the rice isoamylase ISA1-ISA2 heterocomplex

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm; leaf; seeds and leaves discussed
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycoside hydrolase family 13 (GH13); N-terminal domain (NTD); CBM48; amylase domain; C-terminal segment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed except the endosperm; higher than ISA1 in leaf according to cited/integrated analyses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High outside endosperm; implicated in transitory starch synthesis in leaf
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances OsISA1 debranching activity and amylopectin binding through heterocomplex formation; catalytically inactive itself; promotes conversion of soluble phytoglycogen into semi-crystalline amylopectin/starch
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro co-expression/reconstitution of OsISA1-OsISA2 complex; analytical ultracentrifugation; cryo-EM structures of ligand-free and MOS-bound heterocomplex; BCA debranching assays; native PAGE activity assay; pull-down/interface mutagenesis; amylopectin-binding assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsISA2 forms a stable heterocomplex with OsISA1, mainly a 2:1 heterotrimer, and increases thermal stability of the complex. 2. OsISA2 alone is enzymatically inactive on amylopectin, amylose and glycogen, and catalytic-triad restoration does not recover activity. 3. OsISA2 markedly enhances OsISA1 debranching activity (~4-fold in rice assays) and has stronger amylopectin-binding activity than OsISA1. 4. Multiple OsISA1-OsISA2 interfaces were structurally defined and validated by deletion/mutagenesis. 5. ISA2 function in heterocomplex activation is evolutionarily conserved across rice, maize, potato and Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although structurally similar to ISA1, ISA2 is nonenzymatic and appears to trade catalytic capacity for substrate-binding and complex-stabilizing functions. Minor heterotetrameric states were suggested but not structurally resolved at high resolution. OsISA2 contains an extra NTD with amylopectin-binding activity.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro incubation with amylopectin, glycogen, amylose, maltopentaose, maltoheptaose; structural studies after amylopectin incubation captured MOS-bound product state
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RiceXPro spatio-temporal expression database for OsISA1/OsISA2 (no accession given beyond gene pages)
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Catalytically inactive itself; no direct evidence of yield penalty or adverse pleiotropy reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for starch-quality engineering via stacking/co-expression with ISA1 to improve amylopectin biosynthesis and reduce phytoglycogen; species context matters because ISA2 dependence differs among crops
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major storage carbohydrate important for human diet and industrial use; semi-crystalline starch formation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms heterocomplex with ZmISA1 and strongly enhances debranching activity relative to ZmISA1 alone, supporting amylopectin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/purification of ZmISA1-ZmISA2 complex; AUC; native PAGE; enzymatic assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmISA2 forms a conserved ISA1-ISA2 heterocomplex with ZmISA1. 2. The heterocomplex shows higher activity than ZmISA1 alone. 3. ISA2 likely improves ISA1 stability/expression and contributes to amylopectin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes maize endosperm can synthesize starch without strong ISA2-loss phenotype, indicating species/tissue-dependent requirement.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro debranching assays on glucan substrates
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering target with ZmISA1 for starch trait modification

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required in heterocomplex with AtISA1 for detectable debranching activity; promotes amylopectin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/purification of AtISA1-AtISA2 complex; AUC; native PAGE; enzymatic assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtISA2 forms a trimeric ISA1-ISA2 complex with AtISA1. 2. The heterocomplex, but not AtISA1 alone, shows detectable debranching activity in vitro. 3. Results support essential coordinated action of AtISA1 and AtISA2 in amylopectin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Consistent with prior genetic evidence that disruption of either ISA1 or ISA2 gives similar phenotypes in Arabidopsis.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro debranching assays on glucan substrates
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for understanding transitory starch engineering, though this study is mechanistic rather than translational

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Forms active heterocomplex with StISA1; heterocomplex has strong debranching activity whereas StISA1 alone is inactive, promoting amylopectin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/purification of StISA1-StISA2 complex; AUC; native PAGE; enzymatic assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StISA2 assembles with StISA1 into higher-order heteromultimeric complexes. 2. The heterocomplex shows stronger activity than StISA1 alone, which is inactive alone in vitro. 3. Findings support conserved ISA2-assisted amylopectin synthesis in potato.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potato complex appeared mainly as heteromultimers with no obvious StISA1 homodimer on native PAGE, suggesting species-specific oligomeric behavior.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro debranching assays on glucan substrates
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuber starch-quality engineering through coordinated manipulation with StISA1

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycoside hydrolase family 13 (GH13); CBM48; alpha-amylase catalytic domain; C-terminal dimerization domain
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Debranched glucan chains / malto-oligosaccharides; promotes semi-crystalline amylopectin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,6-glycosidic branch linkages in pre-amylopectin or glycogen-like glucans, trimming excess branches to favor amylopectin crystallization
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream remodeling/debranching step during amylopectin assembly
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage-product formation by removing excess branches that otherwise lead to phytoglycogen instead of starch granules
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for phase transition from soluble phytoglycogen to semi-crystalline amylopectin; bottleneck-like branch-trimming determinant
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Active debranching enzyme that promotes amylopectin biosynthesis and prevents excessive branching/phytoglycogen accumulation; activity is strongly enhanced by OsISA2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cryo-EM structure of homodimer and heterocomplex; alanine mutagenesis of catalytic triad (D432/E488/D561); BCA debranching assays; native PAGE activity assays; AUC; substrate-binding assays; interface mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsISA1 forms an active homodimer through a C-terminal dimerization domain. 2. OsISA1 hydrolyzes amylopectin and glycogen but not amylose. 3. The catalytic residues D432, E488, and D561 are essential for debranching activity. 4. OsISA2 binding forms a mainly 2:1 heterotrimer and increases OsISA1 activity about four-fold. 5. OsISA1 is the catalytic engine that trims excess branches to drive conversion of soluble phytoglycogen to semi-crystalline amylopectin.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in ovary and endosperm, low in other tissues according to RiceXPro cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in reproductive/storage tissues; lower in leaf than OsISA2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endosperm; starch granules in seed tissues; also transitory starch context in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with amylopectin, glycogen, amylose; amylopectin incubation for product-bound cryo-EM state
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RiceXPro spatio-temporal expression database for OsISA1/OsISA2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function expected to impair starch granule formation and cause phytoglycogen accumulation; no new agronomic tradeoff directly tested here
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for starch-structure engineering; co-manipulation with ISA2 may optimize amylopectin architecture and granule formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major plant storage carbohydrate relevant to human diet and industrial applications
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cryo-EM resolved homodimer and heterotrimer states; minor heterotetramer likely exists. Dimerization domain is important for protein stability and activity.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Debranched glucan chains enabling amylopectin crystallization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,6 branch points in pre-amylopectin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required to channel glucan flux toward semi-crystalline amylopectin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for proper amylopectin structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmISA1 alone has some activity, but ZmISA1-ZmISA2 heterocomplex is substantially more active, promoting amylopectin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/purification; AUC; native PAGE; enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmISA1 is dimeric in solution. 2. ZmISA1 alone has detectable debranching activity. 3. Complex formation with ZmISA2 strongly increases activity, indicating conserved cooperative function.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: ISA1 higher in endosperm than ISA2, as discussed from prior work
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm-enriched relative to ISA2 in cited context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for cereal starch-property engineering with ISA2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports species-specific partial independence from ISA2 in maize endosperm.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,6 linkages in pre-amylopectin, but activity is only observed in complex with ISA2 under this study's assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for amylopectin formation through heterocomplex with ISA2
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step; functional only in heterocomplex under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtISA1 alone shows no detectable activity, whereas AtISA1-AtISA2 heterocomplex is strongly active, supporting amylopectin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/purification; AUC; native PAGE; enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtISA1 is dimeric in solution. 2. AtISA1 alone has no detectable activity in vitro. 3. Heterocomplex formation with AtISA2 is required for strong debranching activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mechanistic target for transitory starch biology
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Consistent with reported identical phenotypes of isa1 and isa2 mutants in Arabidopsis.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes alpha-1,6 linkages in pre-amylopectin, with activity observed primarily in heterocomplex with ISA2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for amylopectin formation via heterocomplex-assisted branch trimming
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: StISA1 alone is inactive in vitro, while StISA1-StISA2 heterocomplex is strongly active and supports amylopectin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression/purification; AUC; native PAGE; enzymatic assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StISA1 is dimeric by AUC. 2. StISA1 alone shows no detectable activity in vitro. 3. Complex formation with StISA2 generates active heteromultimers important for amylopectin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for potato starch-quality engineering with ISA2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potato ISA1-ISA2 complex displayed distinct oligomeric behavior relative to rice/maize/Arabidopsis.
